item.title: Hackers Used AI to Build Zero-Day Attack, Google Researchers Say
item.description: Security researchers at Alphabet Inc.’s Google said they believe a cybercrime group used artificial intelligence to create a hacking tool that can bypass defenses in a widely-used tool to administer computer systems.

item.title: Verizon Kicks Off US Dollar High-Grade Bond Sale to Refinance
item.description: Verizon Communications Inc. is tapping the US dollar investment-grade debt market as part of a push to refinance debt.

item.title: India Considering Emergency Measures to Save Foreign Exchange
item.description: India is considering emergency steps to shore up foreign-exchange reserves, including curbing non-essential imports like gold and electronic goods, and hiking fuel prices, to help cushion the economy from the fallout of the Iran war, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Europeans Shrug Off High Oil Prices and Keep Burning Fuel
item.description: There’s little sign of widespread oil demand destruction in Europe, despite the sharp rise in wholesale prices caused by the Iran war.

item.title: Argentina Upgrade Seen Opening Narrow Window for Debt Sale
item.description: Argentina’s latest credit upgrade is fueling bets that the country will get another shot at tapping international markets after missing a window in early 2026.

item.title: Shein, Temu Trade Blows as UK Trial Spotlights Supply Chains
item.description: Chinese e-commerce giant Shein and rival Temu have swapped legal blows at the start of a UK trial that’s expected to shine a light on the world of ultra-fast fashion.

item.title: AI Chipmaker Cerebras Systems Seeks $4.8 Billion in Upsized IPO
item.description: Cerebras Systems Inc. increased the size of its initial public offering, now seeking to raise as much as $4.8 billion, as demand for the artificial intelligence chipmaker and data center operator’s shares continues to build.

item.title: SoftBank’s Son in Talks for Major Data Center Project in France
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. founder Masayoshi Son has held talks about unveiling an ambitious French AI data center project with President Emmanuel Macron in the coming weeks, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Investment Bank Lincoln International Seeks $421 Million in IPO
item.description: Mid-market investment bank Lincoln International Inc. and some of its backers are seeking to raise $421 million in a US initial public offering.

item.title: Deutsche Bank Hires Daniel Ghali as Head of Metals Research
item.description: Deutsche Bank AG hired Daniel Ghali to head its metals research department as the lender continues to gradually expand in a business it all but exited a decade ago.

item.title: Brazil Economists Raise 2027 Selic Forecast as Inflation Simmers
item.description: Brazil economists raised their interest rate forecast for next year as inflation comes under pressure from resilient domestic demand and an energy shock caused by the Iran war.

item.title: How a Jet Fuel Supply Crunch Threatens Summer Flights
item.description: The Iran war has squeezed global supplies of jet fuel, threatening to upend vacation plans as the peak summer travel season approaches in the Northern Hemisphere.

item.title: Fermi Wins Support Of Major Shareholder Amid Fight With Ex-CEO
item.description: Fermi Inc. has won the backing of its second-largest shareholder as its former chief executive officer seeks a special meeting to seize control of the data-center developer’s board.

item.title: Novo Hands Over Parkinson’s Therapy to Zuckerberg-Backed Startup
item.description: Novo Nordisk A/S agreed to hand over an experimental therapy for Parkinson’s disease to a Mark Zuckerberg-backed AI startup to potentially speed its development.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 11, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures are little changed as of 7:44 a.m. in New York after Friday’s rally. The US and Iran remain far apart on a framework to end their war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: BP Is Winning Back Stock Analysts as Buy Ratings Double
item.description: A year ago, BP Plc shares were tumbling and stock analysts were telling clients to steer clear. Now it’s becoming a darling on the street, with buy ratings starting to pile up.

item.title: UK Carbon Gains as Starmer Speech Boosts Outlook for EU Linking
item.description: UK carbon futures jumped on Monday after Prime Minister Keir Starmer delivered a speech in which he promised closer ties with the European Union.

item.title: Retail Is Flooding Into the Chipmaker Rally as Moves Get Extreme
item.description: Retail traders largely sat out a record-setting advance in chip stocks in April. Now they’re diving in just as worries mount that the group’s rally may be losing steam.

item.title: Microsoft’s Marquee African Data Center Hits Political Stalemate
item.description: The US tech firm, UAE partner G42 and the Kenyan government appear at an impasse

item.title: Why Scott Bessent Is Stopping in Japan En Route to China
item.description: The Treasury Secretary has a deep knowledge of Japan and its markets

item.title: Blackstone and Halliburton Said to Invest $1 Billion in VoltaGrid
item.description: Blackstone Inc. and Halliburton Co. are investing a combined $1 billion into VoltaGrid, an energy startup that makes gas-powered microgrids that are used to power data centers.

item.title: Starmer Opens Path Toward Full Nationalization of British Steel
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer pledged a step toward full nationalization of British Steel during a crucial speech on Monday designed to save his premiership.

item.title: Circle’s First-Quarter Revenue Increases 20%, Net Income Drops
item.description: Circle Internet Group Inc.’s first-quarter revenue increased 20% while net income declined, as ongoing volatility in cryptocurrency markets roiled earnings from companies across the industry at the start of the year.

item.title: JPMorgan’s Peters Says Inflation Risks Lurk Beneath Stock Peaks
item.description: Global fragmentation, artificial intelligence, and inflation are three defining forces shaping markets which have gained urgency due to recent events such as the war in the Middle East, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co.’s Grace Peters.

item.title: Bank of America Tightened Block Trade Rules After Esprit Deal
item.description: Bank of America Corp. adopted new block‑trade guidelines months after a deal now at the center of a criminal trial involving a well-known hedge fund, a Hong Kong court heard Monday.

item.title: Zimbabwe Sovereign Wealth Fund Seeks $250M to Boost Gold Output
item.description: Zimbabwe’s state-owned sovereign wealth fund, Mutapa Investment Fund Ltd., needs $250 million to expand its gold mining operations across the country.

item.title: Rheinmetall and Deutsche Telekom Partner on German Drone Shield
item.description: Germany’s largest defense company and its biggest mobile phone carrier plan to jointly develop a defense shield against drones and acts of sabotage to protect critical infrastructure in their home country.

item.title: Discounts on Russian Oil Widened for First Time Since Iran War
item.description: Discounts on Russia’s flagship crude widened for the first time since the start of the Iran war, as shifting expectations over a possible end to the Middle East conflict rattled oil markets.

item.title: Behind the Claude Frenzy That Ate Up All the Mac Minis
item.description: Apple’s entry-level desktop has become a vital piece of equipment for people using tools like OpenClaw to roll their own AIs for professional and personal tasks.

item.title: Dream Finders Homes Is Said to Bid $704 Million for Rival Beazer
item.description: Dream Finders Homes Inc. is close to announcing a $704 million offer to acquire rival homebuilder Beazer Homes USA Inc., according to people familiar with the matter.
